title: Memcached Memory Utilization Against limit_maxbytes
app: Custom scripted input (`stats` command) or metrics pipeline
spl:
index=cache sourcetype="memcached:stats"
| eval mem_pct=if(limit_maxbytes>0, round(bytes/limit_maxbytes*100,2), null())
| timechart span=5m max(mem_pct) as used_pct by host
| where used_pct > 90
description: `bytes` approaching `limit_maxbytes` drives evictions and elevated miss latency. Tracking utilization avoids surprise memory pressure.
value: Prevents cache thrash and latency cliffs as datasets grow.
implementation: Poll `stats` every minute; map counters to fields. Correlate with `evictions` and `curr_items`.
visualization: Gauge (used %), line chart (bytes vs limit), overlay eviction rate.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom scripted input (`stats` command) or metrics pipeline.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cache` `sourcetype=memcached:stats` (`bytes`, `limit_maxbytes`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Confirm `limit_maxbytes` is non-zero for your instance; some builds report 0 when unset‚Äîhandle with `eval`.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cache sourcetype="memcached:stats"
| eval mem_pct=if(limit_maxbytes>0, round(bytes/limit_maxbytes*100,2), null())
| timechart span=5m max(mem_pct) as used_pct by host
| where used_pct > 90
```

Understanding this SPL

**Memcached Memory Utilization Against limit_maxbytes** ‚Äî See the description and value fields in this use case JSON.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cache` `sourcetype=memcached:stats` (`bytes`, `limit_maxbytes`). **App/TA**: Custom scripted input (`stats` command) or metrics pipeline. Rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` to match your deployment.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scope to the documented index and sourcetype, then apply transforms, thresholds, and `timechart`/`stats` as in the SPL above.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm events exist, field extractions match your format, and thresholds reflect production baselines.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add to a dashboard or alert; document ownership. Suggested visuals: Gauge (used %), line chart (bytes vs limit), overlay eviction rate..
grandmaExplanation: We watch how full your memory cache is so you can grow it before everything starts getting thrown out.
